page.title: HA / Failover (keepalived)
page.description: VRRP floating-IP failover between patchpanel nodes. Each VRRP instance is a VIP that roams to whichever node has the highest priority (and is healthy). Define VIPs + sync_groups + track_scripts here — these definitions are SHARED across the cluster. Per-node priority/state lives in node.yaml and is edited in the "This node's identity" card below.

toggle.label: Keepalived enabled
toggle.hint: — master switch. When off, the renderer emits a near-empty keepalived.conf regardless of how many instances are configured below.
toggle.disabledWarning: Keepalived is disabled. Configure instances below first, then flip this on to render + reload.

power.managedExternally: Strategy `none` — keepalived is managed outside patchpanel.
power.reloadTitle: Re-read keepalived.conf via SIGHUP. Existing VRRP state is preserved.
power.stopTitle: Stop keepalived. VIPs held by this node will fail over (or vanish). Requires confirmation.
power.startDirectTitle: Direct strategy cannot start keepalived — no supervisor configured.
power.startTitle: Start keepalived via the configured supervisor.

stopConfirm.title: Stop keepalived?
stopConfirm.body1: This will stop keepalived. Any VIPs currently held by this node will fail over to a peer (if one exists with a higher priority). If this is the only node, the VIPs will become unreachable.
stopConfirm.body2: Bring keepalived back up via Start (or your supervisor) once you're ready.

identity.title: This node's identity
identity.description: Edits /etc/patchpanel/node.yaml. These values are local to this node and never sync to peers. Saving triggers a keepalived reload.
identity.nodeIdHint: Human label for this node — surfaces in the peers list on other nodes and in audit entries.
identity.noInstances: No VRRP instances defined yet. Add one in the table above.
identity.removeVipTitle: This node won't participate in this VIP
identity.missingHint: This node won't participate in this VIP until you add a per-node priority/state.

pair.title: Add peer
pair.intro: On the peer node, open this same page and click <1>Mint inbound token</1>. Paste the resulting token here alongside the peer's URL and a friendly display name.<3/>This sets up the <5>outbound</5> direction only (this node → peer). To receive sync pushes from the peer, repeat the flow on the peer's HA page using a token <7>this</7> node minted.
pair.urlHint: Base URL of the peer's patchpanel (same hostname/port you'd use to reach its UI). The peer must be reachable from this host.
pair.nameHint: Human label shown in this node's peer list. Free-form; doesn't need to match the peer's actual node id.
pair.tokenHint: Paste the raw token from the peer's "My inbound tokens" card. It's never re-displayable on the peer side after first reveal.

list.title: Cluster peers
list.description: Outbound peers — patchpanel nodes THIS one pushes state to. State, certs, users, and API tokens sync over a bearer-token API. To add a peer, get an inbound token from the other node's "My inbound tokens" card, then paste it here alongside that node's URL.
list.empty: No peers configured. This node is standalone — VRRP failover requires at least two nodes paired in both directions.
list.skewCritTitle: VRRP may flap — synchronize clocks via chronyd / systemd-timesyncd
list.skewWarnTitle: Approaching VRRP advert_int — check time sync
list.syncTitle: Force a state + cert blob sync now
list.unpairTitle: Remove this peer relationship. State on the peer is not touched.

unpairConfirm.title: Unpair this peer?
unpairConfirm.bodySuffix: from this node's peer list. The peer itself is not modified — you'll also need to unpair from the other side. This node will stop receiving sync pushes from this peer.

inbound.title: My inbound tokens
inbound.description: Bearer tokens THIS node will accept from peers. Mint here, copy once, paste on the other node's <1>Add peer</1> modal. Each token is independently revocable; the raw value is never shown again after mint.

list.empty: No inbound tokens minted yet. Click <1>Mint inbound token</1> to create one.

banner.title: Inbound token minted
banner.body: Copy this token now — it will <1>not</1> be shown again. Paste it into the OTHER node's <3>Add peer</3> modal so that node can call this one.
banner.autoLabelSuffix: — rename it inline in the list below.

inbound.revokeTitle: Revoke this inbound token. Any peer using it will start getting 401s.

revokeConfirm.title: Revoke this inbound token?
revokeConfirm.bodySuffix: ? Any peer currently using it will immediately start getting 401 responses. This cannot be undone.
